originalBehavior: The published article opens with a sticky chart in #doubledescent-container whose training and test error state changes as readers move through the five introductory steps.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original step containers, top chart mount, and compiled scrollytelling transitions so the main double-descent narrative still advances in order.
notes: The local route preserves the published DOM structure that the top Scrollama controller expects.
Only metadata, attribution, and provenance-facing shell copy were localized around the shipped runtime.
evidence: The implementation plan treats the top scrollytelling chart in #doubledescent-container and its five intro steps as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage scrolls across the intro steps and verifies the mounted chart state changes under the published runtime.

originalBehavior: The side narrative coordinates a fitted-model view, an error curve, and a complexity slider so the active model complexity changes the scatter fit and error state together.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published #scatter-container, #error-container, #slider-container, and slider-linked updates without rewriting the compiled interaction logic.
notes: The original slider and chart container IDs were preserved so the runtime can continue targeting them directly.
The public page still exposes the published progression from low-complexity models to interpolation-regime fits.
evidence: The implementation plan marks the side narrative scene and complexity slider synchronization as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage scrolls through the side narrative, changes the slider value, and verifies the active model label and fitted line update.

originalBehavior: The published article uses a local animated media asset in the gap section to show how interpolating models change between training points after the interpolation threshold.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original gap section, explanatory copy, and local GIF asset so the visual explanation still lands without upstream fetches.
notes: The gap media remains bundled with the route instead of hotlinking the original site.
The surrounding explanation remains part of the shipped public page.
evidence: The implementation plan treats the interpolation-gap section and its local media asset as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the gap-section media loads from the local route.

originalBehavior: The published route included MLU branding, creator bylines, analytics, and inter-article references that pointed back to the upstream public site.
localStatus: The local route removes those public-shell provenance surfaces, localizes the remaining inter-article links, and uses the shared footer as the single live original-page reference surface.
notes: Docs retain the upstream repo and provenance notes.
The public route now links locally to bias-variance and double-descent2 where local equivalents exist.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks creator names, MLU branding, upstream domains, analytics, and promo links in shipped route code.
The route now exposes only the shared-footer reference link back to the original page.
